analysis.headline: Given ONLY lat/lon (no elevation input, no coords in text), the model predicts elevation-above-median at 0.704 +/- 0.019 — well above chance. The spatial encoder learned a real lat/lon -> elevation mapping and generalizes it to held-out cities.
analysis.three_d_vs_two_d: 0.983 (elevation handed in directly) vs 0.704 (must infer from location). The ~0.28 gap is the shortcut of being given elevation as an input coordinate; location alone still carries substantial signal.
analysis.validates_step1: coord_2d_noleak (0.704) matches the leaky Step-1 coord_2d (0.717) almost exactly, and coord_3d_noleak (0.983) ~ leaky coord_3d (0.974). So Step 1's 2D-vs-3D gap was NOT a text-leak artifact — removing the leak reproduces the same numbers. The original finding holds.
analysis.gates: Gates stayed ~0.002 yet are load-bearing (no text signal exists), consistent with the earlier no-leak run. Gate magnitude is not a usage readout.
analysis.per_module: Per-module gating remains dropped (no benefit + 1/3-seed collapse in the earlier run).
